authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham - OET Speaking Nursing Rulebook (AI Training Edition)

sections.title: OET Speaking Format and Scoring Anchor
Risk Language, Lifestyle Advice, and Follow-Up

rules.title: Speaking pass anchor
rules.body: Speaking practice is interpreted against the OET Grade B anchor: approximately 350 on the 500-point OET scale. Learner feedback is advisory and not an official OET result.

rules.title: Use plain English with patients and carers
rules.body: Avoid unexplained clinical jargon, abbreviations, and ward shorthand when speaking to patients or carers. In clinician-to-clinician handover, expand safety-critical abbreviations the first time they appear.

rules.title: Professional opening and role clarity
rules.body: Open with a calm greeting, identify yourself as the nurse, confirm the listener or patient context, and explain the purpose of the conversation before giving details.
exemplarPhrases: Hello, my name is Nurse Smith. I will be looking after you today.
Hello, I am handing over Mr Wheeler before the next shift begins.

rules.title: Empathy before instruction
rules.body: When a patient or carer expresses worry, pain, embarrassment, or reluctance, acknowledge the feeling before giving instructions or advice.
exemplarPhrases: I am sorry you have been uncomfortable.
That sounds worrying, and I can understand why you would feel concerned.

rules.title: Check understanding
rules.body: After explaining care instructions, medication, mobility advice, or risk precautions, check that the listener has understood and invite questions.
exemplarPhrases: Does that make sense so far?
Would you like me to go over any part again?

rules.title: Summarise the key plan
rules.body: Before closing, give a short summary of the current condition, immediate safety priorities, and next actions.
exemplarPhrases: To recap, the main priorities are pain control, safe mobility, and monitoring for any new symptoms.

rules.title: Close with support and escalation
rules.body: Close warmly and make the next contact or escalation point clear. Patients should know who to call; clinicians should know what still needs action.
exemplarPhrases: Please press the call bell if you are worried or your pain changes.
Please let me know if you need any more detail before you take over.

rules.title: Keep the interaction two-way
rules.body: Do not turn the role play into a long monologue. Pause, invite response, and adapt to the listener's concerns.

rules.title: Ask sensitive questions respectfully
rules.body: Handle weight, hygiene, continence, smoking, alcohol, adherence, and mobility limitations with tact. Avoid blunt or judgmental wording.
exemplarPhrases: Would you mind if I ask a few questions about your usual mobility and general health?

rules.title: Smoking advice starts with cessation
rules.body: When smoking is clinically relevant, begin with clear advice to stop smoking before negotiating reduction or alternatives.

rules.title: Do not over-diagnose from one reading
rules.body: Do not diagnose hypertension, diabetes, or another long-term condition from a single observation or screening result. Explain the finding and the need for follow-up.

rules.title: Nursing handover uses situation, background, assessment, and recommendation
rules.body: For a nursing handover task, organise the message around the current situation, relevant background, present assessment, risks, and clear recommendations or outstanding tasks.
exemplarPhrases: The outstanding tasks for the next shift are...

rules.title: Escalate red flags clearly
rules.body: Mention safety-critical changes such as uncontrolled pain, fever, bleeding, shortness of breath, confusion, falls risk, reduced urine output, or worsening mobility without burying them in minor details.

rules.title: 9 scoreable criteria — scored separately
rules.body: The OET Speaking recording is scored against NINE criteria, not six. Linguistic criteria (4, each 0–6): Intelligibility, Fluency, Appropriateness of Language, Resources of Grammar & Expression. Clinical Communication criteria (5, each 0–3 — descriptors: Adept / Competent / Partially effective / Ineffective): Relationship Building; Understanding & Incorporating the Patient's Perspective; Providing Structure; Information Gathering; Information Giving. Grouping Clinical Communication into a single aggregate score is WRONG — every feedback item must cite one of the nine criterion codes AND a rule ID.

rules.title: Interlocutor does NOT grade
rules.body: The interlocutor is an ACTOR: they facilitate the role play, read warm-up questions, and play the patient/carer. The interlocutor NEVER assesses or scores the candidate.

rules.title: OET Assessor grades the recording
rules.body: A separate OET Assessor listens to the audio recording AFTER the exam and scores it against all 9 criteria. The platform's AI evaluation pipeline must therefore evaluate the recording post-hoc, not live interaction dynamics that are not audible on the recording.

rules.title: Computer-based Speaking is always at home
rules.body: In the computer-based OET, Speaking is always taken at home — never at a test centre. The device check and mic check flow must enforce or clearly inform the candidate of the full environment requirement list (RULE_74) before the exam can start.

rules.title: Computer-based environment requirements
rules.body: Location: at home only. Room: indoors, well-lit, solid walls (no glass panels or transparent partitions). Other people: candidate must be completely alone — if anyone enters the room, the exam is stopped. Monitors: single monitor only. Webcam: wired or built-in webcam that can move; Bluetooth webcam NOT permitted. Microphone & speakers: built-in or USB plug-in only; Bluetooth NOT allowed; NO headsets during Speaking. Power: device plugged directly into power; no docking station; no battery-only. VPN / Virtual machines: fully disabled before the exam starts. Extra screens: all other screens (TV, second monitor) unplugged.

rules.title: Paper + pen permitted; on-screen annotation is NOT
rules.body: Computer-based Speaking: candidates CANNOT highlight or annotate the role-play card on screen. However they ARE permitted ONE blank piece of paper and a pen to take notes during preparation time and during the role play. Paper-based Speaking: candidates may annotate the card with pencil (no separate paper needed).

rules.title: Destroy paper at end of computer-based exam
rules.body: At the end of the computer-based Speaking exam, the candidate MUST tear or cut the blank paper in front of the camera to confirm nothing is removed from the test environment. The preparation screen must explicitly inform the candidate of this before the exam starts.

laymanGlossary.example: We are preventing blood clots in the legs.
This pain medicine is available when needed.
We will keep checking your blood pressure.

laymanGlossary.plain: not eating or drinking for now
laymanGlossary.example: Please do not eat or drink until the doctor confirms it is safe.
We will help you get up and walk safely.
The tube helps drain urine from your bladder.

consultationStages.name: greeting
consultationStages.what: Greet, identify your nursing role, and set the purpose.

consultationStages.name: listening
consultationStages.what: Invite concerns and listen without interrupting.
consultationStages.example: Tell me what is worrying you most.

consultationStages.name: empathy
consultationStages.example: I am sorry you have been in pain.

consultationStages.name: information
consultationStages.what: Explain the current situation in plain English.
consultationStages.example: Your recovery is progressing, and we are watching your pain and mobility.

consultationStages.name: safety_plan
consultationStages.example: Please call us straight away if you feel short of breath.

consultationStages.name: closure
consultationStages.what: Close warmly with next contact or outstanding tasks.
consultationStages.example: Please press the call bell if you need help.

smokingLadder.name: support
smokingLadder.what: Offer cessation support or nicotine replacement if appropriate.

smokingLadder.name: reduction
smokingLadder.what: Discuss reduction only after cessation advice has been given.
